description: We are responsible for the administration of criminal, civil and family courts in England and Wales, as well as the reserved unified tribunals across the United Kingdom. HMCTS is an executive agency, sponsored by the Ministry of Justice .

details.body:
We are responsible for the administration of criminal, civil and family courts in England and Wales, as well as the reserved unified tribunals across the United Kingdom.

HMCTS is an executive agency, sponsored by the Ministry of Justice.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Craig Robb, Deputy Director and Crime Live Service Owner at HMCTS, reflects on the past year in the criminal courts – what’s been achieved, what needs to change and why he remains confident in the direction of travel.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Criminal courts: building on progress, rising to the challenge

image.alt_text: One person in a dark suit and striped tie stands by a railing in a bright, modern atrium with blurred displays behind.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Sir Richard Broadbent has been reappointed as Chair of the His Majesty’s Courts and Tribunals Service (HMCTS) Board.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Sir Richard Broadbent reappointed as Chair of the HMCTS Board

image.alt_text: Business professional presenting holographic legal and AI icons, including scales of justice, law book, people, shield, and courthouse.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: A new study will explore how AI can be used to transcribe court hearings and open the door to faster, cheaper access to court records for victims.
ordered_featured_documents.title: AI court transcripts to boost access to justice for victims

ordered_featured_documents.summary: HMCTS issues regular bulletins to provide the latest information and update on progress across our estate.
ordered_featured_documents.title: HM Courts and Tribunals Service information bulletins

ordered_featured_documents.summary: In our latest episode of Inside HMCTS, we go behind the scenes at Manchester Magistrates Court, spending a day with dedicated GeoAmey staff to see firsthand what it takes to move thousands of people safely through the justice system every week.
ordered_featured_documents.title: From Cell to Court: Inside Prisoner Escort and Custody Services

ordered_featured_documents.summary: HMCTS Chief Executive Nick Goodwin reflects on how the organisation has built strong foundations for continued transformation, the challenges that remain, and what the future holds.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Modernising services: Nick Goodwin’s reflections on change at HMCTS

ordered_featured_links.title: Make a court claim for money
What to do when someone dies

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Read about the types of information we routinely publish in our Publication scheme. Find out about our commitment to publishing in Welsh. Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use.

details.body:
The Public Guardian and Accounting Officer is responsible to the Lord Chancellor and the Secretary of State for Justice for the operation of the OPG, including the way it spends public money and manages its assets.

The Public Guardian is appointed by the Lord Chancellor under Section 57 of the Mental Capacity Act 2005 (MCA) and their responsibilities extend throughout England and Wales.

role_appointments.title: Nick Goodwin - Public Guardian and Chief Executive

details.body: The Chief Executive is responsible for: overall leadership the delivery of services strategy and vision The role is also the Accounting Officer for the organisation.

role.title: Chief Executive and Board Member, HM Courts & Tribunals Service

role_appointments.title: Nick Goodwin - Chief Executive and Board member, HM Courts & Tribunals Service

details.body:
The HMCTS Chief Finance Officer is responsible for:

  • financial plans for day-to-day operations
  • strategic objectives
  • financial management, governance and controls
  • property management

role.title: Chief Finance Officer and Board Member

role_appointments.title: Catherine Blair - Chief Finance Officer and Board Member
Annabel Burns - Strategy, Analysis and Planning Director

details.body: Annabel Burns joined the board of HM Courts & Tribunals Service as a representative of the Ministry of Justice in August 2019

role.title: Ministry of Justice Representative Board Member

role_appointments.title: Annabel Burns - Ministry of Justice Representative Board Member

details.body: Responsible for operational delivery of all HMCTS court, tribunal and national services and HMCTS’ implementation of criminal court reforms from the Independent Review of the Criminal Courts.

The Head of Criminal Casework leads the teams that process all applications and bills for criminal legal aid and central funds. This also includes the collection and enforcement of contributions made under the Crown Court means testing scheme.

The post-holder is a member of the Executive Leadership Team, chairs the LAA’s Operational Committee and has membership of the LAA board.

role_appointments.title: Daniel Flury - Head of Criminal Casework

details.body: The Human Resources Director is responsible for HMCTS workforce strategy covering capacity, capability and colleague experience.

The Chief Digital and Information Officer is responsible for Information Technology and Digital Strategy, which includes designing the infrastructure to support reforms to the service and ensuring the continued evolution and running of the current IT services.

role_appointments.title: John Laverick - Chief Digital and Information Officer

details.body:
The Courts and Tribunals Development Director is responsible for developing the service models for:

  • crime, civil, family and tribunals
  • national performance

This will include sharing thinking on streamlining and improving IT, back office planning and implementation.

ordered_roles.title: Courts and Tribunals Development Director and Board Member

details.body: The Chief Digital and Information Officer is responsible for Information Technology and Digital Strategy, which includes designing the infrastructure to support reforms to the service and ensuring the continued evolution and running of the current IT services.

The HMCTS Chief Finance Officer is responsible for:

  • financial plans for day-to-day operations
  • strategic objectives
  • financial management, governance and controls
  • property management

ordered_roles.title: Chief Finance Officer and Board Member
Independent Chair of Audit Committee (Non-executive Board Member)

details.body: The Customer Director is responsible for enabling HMCTS to become a customer-led organisation. By understanding the needs of citizens, and the impact a case may have on their lives, HMCTS will be better placed to help people to participate in justice services with confidence.
The Commercial Director will ensure the organisation uses innovative, efficient and effective commercial arrangements to deliver its reform programme.
The Chief Executive is responsible for: overall leadership the delivery of services strategy and vision The role is also the Accounting Officer for the organisation.

ordered_roles.title: Chief Executive and Board Member, HM Courts & Tribunals Service

details.body:
Responsible for:

  • managing the transition from old to reformed ways of working
  • balancing Reform delivery with organisational stability
  • linking people and service transition
  • managing the realisation of FTE benefits
Responsible for ensuring that the crime programme is delivered to meet the needs of our users and partners across the criminal justice system.

The National Services Director is responsible and accountable for:

  • customer service centres, CTSCs and National Business Centres (NBCs) for HMCTS ensuring these provide a good service and deliver Reform programme efficiencies. The service centres process ‘out of court’ activity for family, civil and tribunals cases as well as crime cases in the magistrates’ courts. They also handle calls, email and webchat from citizens and legal professionals and support the judiciary.
  • the CTSCs deal with reformed work while the NBCs process unreformed and legacy cases. The centres are being led on an end-to-end jurisdiction basis working with colleagues in courts and tribunals and across HMCTS.

The HR Director is responsible for:

  • talent development
  • staff engagement
  • diversity

The role is also responsible for the workforce strategy, development and people change across the organisation.

ordered_roles.title: Human Resources Director and Board Member

details.body: Responsible for operational delivery of all HMCTS court, tribunal and national services and HMCTS’ implementation of criminal court reforms from the Independent Review of the Criminal Courts.
The Human Resources Director is responsible for HMCTS workforce strategy covering capacity, capability and colleague experience.

title: HM Courts & Tribunals Service
